reliability: High (Tether official, CoinDesk, Fortune, The Block)
scanType: Focused scan: Stablecoin Regulation & USDT Risk (LEVER collateral dependency)
summary: GENIUS Act signed July 2025: first US federal stablecoin law. Requires 100% reserve backing with liquid assets, monthly public disclosures, AML/KYC. Final rules by July 2026. Issuers over $50B in liabilities need full audits.
Tether hired KPMG to audit $185B USDT reserves and PwC for internal systems (March 27, 2026). 80%+ of reserves now in US Treasury Bills (vs. historical commercial paper). Major transparency upgrade.

Tether launched USAT (USA₮) on January 27, 2026: US-regulated stablecoin issued by Anchorage Digital Bank (OCC-chartered). Cantor Fitzgerald as reserve custodian. On Ethereum. ~20M in circulation, mostly institutional. GENIUS Act compliant. USDT continues globally.

EU MiCA in full enforcement. Only USDC is MiCA-compliant among top 10 stablecoins. USDT not compliant in EU. Authorization deadline July 1, 2026. This creates a USDT risk in EU markets but does not affect Base/US operations.

LEVER uses USDT as sole collateral. USDT is increasingly well-backed ($185B, 80%+ T-Bills, KPMG audit incoming) but faces regulatory bifurcation: USDT for global, USAT for US, USDC for EU. LEVER may need to support multiple stablecoins depending on market reach.

entities.name: Scott Bessent

entities.role: EU crypto regulation, full enforcement. USDT non-compliant. Deadline July 1 2026.

relationships.relationship: requires 100% reserve, monthly disclosures, full audit for >$50B issuers
USDT not compliant; may be restricted in EU markets

leverCollateralRisk.currentState: LEVER uses USDT as sole collateral in LeverVault (ERC-4626)
positiveSignals: USDT reserves now 80%+ US Treasury Bills (high quality)
GENIUS Act provides regulatory clarity (better than gray zone)
Tether launching USAT for US compliance shows proactive adaptation
$185B in reserves; systemic importance makes regulatory destruction unlikely
risks: Single collateral dependency: any USDT depeg cascades through entire protocol
USDT not MiCA-compliant: EU restrictions possible by July 2026
USAT is on Ethereum only; not clear if it will be on Base
If LEVER targets US users, USAT may be required instead of/alongside USDT
GENIUS Act final rules (July 2026) could impose requirements on DeFi protocols using stablecoins
recommendedActions: BUILD: Design LeverVault to support multiple collateral types (USDT, USDC, USAT) from v2 or via wrapper
ADVISOR: Monitor GENIUS Act final rules (July 2026) for DeFi protocol obligations
CEO: For investors, emphasize USDT's improved transparency (KPMG audit, T-Bill backing) while acknowledging multi-collateral as roadmap item
SECURE: Monitor USDT peg stability as part of ongoing risk framework

workstreamTags.BUILD: LeverVault is USDT-only. For v1 this is fine. For v2/scaling, design should accommodate USDC and potentially USAT. The stablecoin landscape is bifurcating: USDT global, USAT US, USDC EU. Multi-collateral support will be needed for multi-market reach.
workstreamTags.CEO: Good news: USDT is safer than ever ($185B, 80% T-Bills, KPMG audit). The GENIUS Act provides regulatory clarity. Bad news: single collateral dependency is a risk investors will ask about. Answer: 'v1 is USDT for simplicity and liquidity. Multi-collateral (USDC, USAT) is on the roadmap for geographic expansion.'
workstreamTags.ADVISOR: July 2026 is a key regulatory date: both GENIUS Act final rules AND MiCA enforcement deadline. LEVER mainnet (Q2 2026) should launch before these deadlines to establish market position. Multi-collateral should be prioritized for Q3-Q4.
workstreamTags.SECURE: USDT depeg risk is low but non-zero. Insurance fund (20% of fees) provides some buffer. A USDT depeg scenario should be part of the risk framework.

sources.title: CoinDesk: Tether Hires KPMG for USDT Audit
